tldr: Twilio and Vonage are both REST-based CPaaS providers covering SMS, voice, and video, so a migration is largely an SDK swap, webhook rework, and number porting. Plan extra time for porting numbers and for re-approval of any WhatsApp or voice routing.
why_switch: Alternative carrier routing and pricing mix
what_to_export: Studio flows or other low-code workflows
export_method: Build an inventory using Twilio's REST API: phone numbers, Messaging Services, sender pools, Studio flows, TwiML applications, sub-accounts, API keys, and webhook URLs. Numbers have to be ported with a Letter of Authorization rather than copied, so request these early. For voice, export TwiML XML and any externally hosted bins; for messaging, export templates and copy approved WhatsApp senders. Save webhook signing secrets so you can decommission them cleanly later. Pull a few weeks of message and call logs if you need them for analytics or compliance. None of this needs a Twilio-specific feature beyond the standard REST API.
import_method: Open a Vonage account, create applications and API keys, and submit number ports early. Re-implement IVR and call control logic using Vonage's NCCO (Nexmo Call Control Object) JSON model rather than TwiML. Recreate Messaging API senders and WhatsApp templates (Meta will re-approve templates against your new business account). Update SDK calls and webhook handlers, including signature validation. Run staging tests with a Vonage test number, then move a small slice of production traffic first. Keep Twilio active during porting so traffic is not dropped. Once porting completes and metrics are stable, decommission Twilio numbers and credentials.

concept_mapping.target: No direct equivalent; rebuild as code or third-party flow

effort: 3-8 weeks, dominated by number porting and IVR rebuild
gotchas: TwiML does not run on Vonage; voice/IVR logic must be rebuilt in NCCO or your own code
Number porting per country can take days to weeks and requires LOAs
WhatsApp Business senders and templates must be re-approved by Meta
Webhook signature validation differs and must be re-implemented
Per-region pricing differs; rerun cost modeling on your actual traffic mix
Run dual-send during cutover and compare delivery rates before fully switching

email.subject: Migrating CPaaS from Twilio to Vonage over the next 14 days
email.body:
Hi team,

We are migrating from Twilio to Vonage over the next 14 days. The APIs are similar but voice control is rebuilt and numbers must be ported.

Days 1-3: inventory numbers, IVR flows, templates, and webhooks.
Days 4-6: open Vonage account, kick off number porting, submit WhatsApp templates.
Days 7-10: rebuild IVR in NCCO, swap SDKs, rewrite webhook validation.
Days 11-14: dual-send a slice of traffic, then cut over once delivery and call quality look healthy.

Some ports may extend beyond 14 days; we will keep Twilio active until porting completes.

Thanks,
Your customer
